contents.value: **let** — Power Query M keyword.
**in** — Power Query M keyword.
**if** — Power Query M keyword.
**then** — Power Query M keyword.
**else** — Power Query M keyword.
**Table.FromRecords** — Built-in function/command in Power Query M.
**Table.SelectRows** — Built-in function/command in Power Query M.
**Table.AddColumn** — Built-in function/command in Power Query M.
**Table.TransformColumns** — Built-in function/command in Power Query M.
**Table.RemoveColumns** — Built-in function/command in Power Query M.

```sql
SELECT column1, column2 FROM table WHERE condition
```
**SELECT** retrieves data from one or more tables.

```sql
INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2)
```
**INSERT** adds new rows to a table.

```sql
UPDATE table SET col = val WHERE condition
```
**UPDATE** modifies existing rows.

```sql
DELETE FROM table WHERE condition
```
**DELETE** removes rows from a table.

```sql
SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id
```
**JOIN** combines rows from multiple tables based on a related column.

An **index** speeds up queries by creating an optimized lookup structure.
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx ON table(column);
```

A **view** is a virtual table defined by a query.
```sql
CREATE VIEW name AS SELECT ...;
```

A **transaction** groups operations that must succeed or fail as a unit.
```sql
BEGIN; ... COMMIT; -- or ROLLBACK;
```

**Aggregate functions** compute a single result from a set of rows.
```sql
COUNT(*), SUM(col), AVG(col), MIN(col), MAX(col)
```

A **subquery** is a query nested inside another query.
```sql
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM t2)
```

A **Common Table Expression** defines a named temporary result set.
```sql
WITH cte AS (SELECT ...) SELECT * FROM cte
```

**Constraints** enforce rules on data in tables.
```sql
PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, CHECK
```
**NULL** represents the absence of a value. Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL to test.

**Window functions** compute values across a set of rows related to the current row.
```sql
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col)
```

**GROUP BY** groups rows with the same values for aggregate calculations.
```sql
SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM emp GROUP BY dept
```

```powerquery
Table.Group(table as table, key as any, aggregatedColumns as list, optional groupKind, optional comparer) -> table
```
Groups rows by the specified key columns and applies aggregate operations such as sum, count, and list to the remaining columns, equivalent to SQL GROUP BY functionality.

```powerquery
Table.Join(table1 as table, key1 as any, table2 as table, key2 as any, optional joinKind as JoinKind.Type, optional joinAlgorithm, optional keyEqualityComparers) -> table
```
Combines rows from two tables based on matching key values, supporting inner, left outer, right outer, full outer, left anti, and right anti join operations.

```powerquery
List.Transform(list as list, transform as function) -> list
```
Applies a transformation function to each element of the input list and returns a new list containing the transformed values, enabling functional mapping operations on collections.

```powerquery
List.Select(list as list, selection as function) -> list
```
Returns a new list containing only the elements from the input list where the selection function returns true, providing predicate-based filtering for list values.

```powerquery
List.Accumulate(list as list, seed as any, accumulator as function) -> any
```
Reduces a list to a single value by applying an accumulator function that takes the current state and each element, producing a running aggregation similar to fold or reduce.

```powerquery
Record.Field(record as record, field as text) -> any
```
Returns the value of the specified field from the given record. Raises an error if the field does not exist, unlike the optional Record.FieldOrDefault which handles missing fields.

```powerquery
Record.TransformFields(record as record, transformOperations as list, optional missingField) -> record
```
Returns a new record with the specified fields transformed by applying the provided functions, enabling selective modification of record field values during data cleaning.

```powerquery
Text.Contains(text as nullable text, substring as text, optional comparer as function) -> logical
```
Returns true if the text value contains the specified substring. Supports an optional comparer function for case-insensitive or culture-aware string matching operations.

```powerquery
Text.Combine(texts as list, optional separator as nullable text) -> text
```
Concatenates a list of text values into a single string with an optional separator between each element, useful for building delimited strings from list-based data.

```powerquery
Number.From(value as any, optional culture as nullable text) -> nullable number
```
Converts a text, logical, date, time, or other value to a number using the specified culture for locale-aware parsing of decimal separators and number formatting conventions.

```powerquery
Type.Is(type1 as type, type2 as type) -> logical
```
Returns true if type1 is compatible with type2, used for runtime type checking and conditional logic based on the data types of values flowing through query transformations.

```powerquery
each expression
```
Shorthand syntax in Power Query M for creating a single-parameter function where the underscore represents the parameter. Equivalent to writing (_) => expression for concise lambda definitions.

```powerquery
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```powerquery
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```powerquery
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```powerquery
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```powerquery
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```powerquery
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.
